steps.title: Identify the shape as linear
steps.content: The graph displays a straight line, not a curve. Linear functions have constant rate of change and form straight lines, while exponential functions form curves. Since the graph is straight, the function must be linear. This eliminates options C and D (both exponential).

steps.title: Determine the direction
steps.content:
Reading the graph from left to right, as x increases, the y-values increase as well. The line rises upward from lower-left to upper-right. This indicates the function is increasing, not decreasing, which eliminates choice B.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
